title: thelypodium auriculatum
intro: Thelypodium auriculatum, also known as Golden Ears or Golden Eared Thelypody, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The herbal preparation of Thelypodium auriculatum typically involves drying the leaves and stems, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized.
